task_completion.description: Verify that the required layers (FRAME, SHELVES, TEXT, DIMENSIONS) are created with the correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the LibreCAD Layer List panel (usually on the right), look for layers named 'FRAME' (white/black icon), 'SHELVES' (green icon), 'TEXT' (cyan icon), and 'DIMENSIONS' (red icon).

task_completion.description: Verify the outer frame and header panel are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the drawing area, look for a large rectangular frame and a horizontal rectangular panel at the top (header), drawn in white/black.

task_completion.description: Verify the two side vertical panels are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the drawing area, look for two vertical rectangles on the left and right sides, extending from the bottom to the header panel, drawn in white/black.

task_completion.description: Verify the two horizontal shelves are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the drawing area, look for two horizontal lines drawn in green, spanning the space between the two side panels.

task_completion.description: Verify the text 'ACME DISPLAYS' is added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'ACME DISPLAYS' in cyan color, placed inside the top header panel.
Look for red dimension lines and text indicating the total width (3000) and total height (2500) of the booth.

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a save dialog or the LibreCAD window title bar showing the file saved as 'booth_elevation.dxf' in the '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used LibreCAD to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD application interface being actively used to draw and configure layers, not just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually drew the elements or used LibreCAD commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with LibreCAD's drawing tools (lines, rectangles, text, dimensions) or command line interface at the bottom of the LibreCAD window.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a Python script to generate the DXF file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python script (e.g., using ezdxf) in a terminal or IDE to bypass using LibreCAD.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a LibreCAD drawing with specific layers and colors: FRAME (white/black), SHELVES (green), TEXT (cyan), and DIMENSIONS (red). The drawing must include a 3000x2500 outer frame, a 3000x400 header panel, two 200x2100 side panels, two horizontal shelves at y=700 and y=1400, cyan text 'ACME DISPLAYS' in the header, and red dimensions for 3000 width and 2500 height. The file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/booth_elevation.dxf.
